data.title: Is Metaplanet Stock a Buy? Profit Surges 81%, but Stock Is Down 63% in 6 Months on Bitcoin Losses
data.description:
Metaplanet (JP:3350), often called the "Strategy of Japan," is sending mixed signals that have investors scratching their heads. The Tokyo-based firm just announced that its core business is booming, with operating profits expected to climb 81% this year. This follows a jaw-dropping 2025 where profits soared 17-fold. However, looking at the ticker tells a different story: the stock has plummeted over 60% in the last six months. For those asking if this is a buy-the-dip moment or a falling knife, the answer lies in the massive gap between the company's operating success and its paper losses.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Metaplanet's Options Writing Strategy Drives Revenue

The company's underlying business is actually a money-making machine right now. Metaplanet reported that its operating profit hit 6.28 billion yen ($40.8 million) last year, driven almost entirely by a clever strategy of writing Bitcoin options. Essentially, they are getting paid high "premiums" just for owning Bitcoin (BTC-USD) and betting on its volatility.

  • The Good News: Revenue jumped 738%, and the company expects to bring in 16 billion yen in 2026.
  • The Bad News: Because Bitcoin's price fell from its $125,000 peak to below $90,000 by the end of the year, Metaplanet had to report a "non-cash valuation loss" of 102.2 billion yen ($650 million). This massive number dragged their total net income into a loss of $605 million, which is exactly why the stock price has been crushed despite the business actually growing.

Management Buys the Dip

While the stock market is panicking, Metaplanet management is doubling down. The company now holds 35,102 BTC, worth more than $2.4 billion, and they have no plans to stop. They recently approved a massive 12.2 billion yen equity offering to buy even more coins. This creates a mismatch between the company's growth and the stock price:

  • The Bull Case: The company is trading at a huge discount because of paper losses that aren't actually realized. If Bitcoin rebounds, those losses vanish, and you're left with a company that has a highly profitable options-trading business.
  • The Bear Case: To keep buying Bitcoin, Metaplanet is issuing millions of new shares. This dilutes current shareholders, meaning each share you own represents a smaller piece of the company.

Key Takeaway

Ultimately, whether Metaplanet is a buy depends on your view of Bitcoin. Overall, Metaplanet is a profitable business wearing a scary mask. On paper, they lost over $600 million, but in reality, they didn't actually lose that cash, their Bitcoin just lost value on the open market. Meanwhile, they are making millions in actual cash by trading options on those coins. If you believe Bitcoin is going back up, the stock looks like a bargain because you're getting the profitable trading business almost for free. But if you hate the idea of your investment being diluted by new share offerings, you might want to watch from the sidelines.

Is Metaplanet Stock a Good Buy?

data.title: Quantum Computing News: $100 Million NSF Boost and $550 Million SPAC Deal Put Infleqtion in the Spotlight
data.description:
Welcome all to Monday's update on quantum computing. As 2026 moves ahead, the story keeps shifting from lab trials toward scale, cash flow, and public market moves. This update covers a new $100 million U.S. network plan, a gold-based research deal, a learner quantum chemistry tool, a major chip packaging step, and a SPAC deal set to close.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Let us begin.

1. NSF Launches $100 million Quantum and Nano Network

We begin in the U.S., where the National Science Foundation is launching a $100 million National Quantum and Nanotechnology Infrastructure program. The plan will fund up to 16 open-access sites over five years, starting Feb. 13, 2026.

These sites will offer tools for chip building, device testing, and hands-on training. The goal is to serve students, small firms, and labs across the country. Don Millard, head of Engineering at NSF, said, "This NSF investment in research facilities will power U.S. discovery in quantum and nanotechnologies to fuel our economy."

In other words, this is a scale play. Rather than fund one lab at a time, the NSF wants a shared system that helps ideas move quickly from early work to real use. The plan also builds on nearly 50 years of prior nano funding, which gives it some policy depth.

2. Delta Gold Funds Penn State in IP Push

Next, Delta Gold Technologies PLC (GB:DGQ) signed a research and license deal with Penn State. The firm will fund up to $2.99 million over three years, with about $997,142 in year one. The work will focus on gold-based quantum tech. In return, Delta will get an exclusive license to any new IP, except in human health. Penn State will receive a 1% royalty on net sales after total sales pass $20 million.

Chief Executive Officer R. Michael Jones said, "Signing an agreement with Penn State adds to our portfolio of potential IP." The firm is also active with the University of Toronto, which shows a cross-campus push. For a small public firm, IP depth can drive long-term value. However, this is still early-stage work, so revenue impact may take time.

3. Xanadu Cuts Quantum Chemistry Costs

Meanwhile, Xanadu Quantum Technologies released a preprint on a new algorithm for quantum chemistry. The firm said the method reduces resource needs by more than an order of magnitude for certain reactions.

Most chemistry tools rely on the Born Oppenheimer rule, which splits nuclear and electron motion. Xanadu's method goes beyond that, which may help in photo reactions used in chip and energy work.

Chief Executive Officer Christian Weedbrook said the tool lowers resource needs and helps position quantum tech for key industrial use. If these cost cuts hold up, they could ease the path toward fault-tolerant systems. For now, this remains a research result. Still, lower compute needs matter for any firm building large-scale hardware.

4. Oxford Quantum Circuits Scales to 500 Qubits

In hardware news, Oxford Quantum Circuits unveiled a wafer-scale chip package that can house more than 500 superconducting qubits on one 3-inch die. The firm said it achieved a median T1 time of 97 microseconds and a T2e time of 129 microseconds across tested qubits. Readout error was about 2.5%, or 97.5% fidelity.

Importantly, the team used energy participation ratio methods to limit microwave loss. The firm said tests show performance is "material limited rather than packaging limited." In this case, OQC claims it can grow qubit count without loss in key metrics. That is a core hurdle on the road to fault-tolerant systems.

5. Infleqtion SPAC Deal Keeps Cash Intact

Finally, Churchill Capital Corp X shareholders approved a merger with Infleqtion Inc., with low share redemptions. As a result, Infleqtion is set to receive more than $550 million in gross proceeds, including nearly 100% of trust cash and more than $125 million from a PIPE.

Redemption rates in recent SPAC deals have often ranged from 70% to 90%. In this case, most holders stayed in. That gives Infleqtion a strong cash base as it enters the public market.

Infleqtion develops neutral-atom quantum systems and sells precision sensing tools. After closing, the firm is set to trade on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker (INFQ).

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to line up the main publicly traded quantum stocks. It is a simple way to see how firms like IonQ (IONQ) and D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) stack up as public funding, lab gains, and capital market moves begin to align.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: XRP ETFs' Sudden Demand Slump Could Cost Ripple Its Top-Four Crown as XRP Price Teeters
data.description:
Institutional investors seem to be taking a breather after three months of aggressive buying into the world's first XRP (XRP-USD) exchange-traded funds. According to data from February 15, 2026, the initial hype that fueled these funds has started to fade, leading to a worrying trend reversal in capital flows. While the funds managed to end the week in the green, the numbers were a far cry from the record-breaking volume seen during their November debut.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Let's Break Down the Quiet Week for XRP ETFs

The market leader, Canary Capital's XRPC (XRPC), still holds the top spot with over $410 million in total inflows, but the daily activity has slowed to a crawl.

  • Monday: The week started with a modest $6.31 million in new money.
  • Tuesday: Interest dipped further to $3.26 million.
  • Wednesday: Trading volume completely flatlined, recording $0 in net flows.
  • Thursday: Investors actually pulled money out, leading to a withdrawal of $6.42 million.
  • Friday: A small bounce brought in $4.5 million to close the week.

XRP Price Defies the ETF Slump

Even as the funds saw declining demand, the actual XRP token spent the weekend on a rollercoaster. The asset managed to claw its way back from a recent low of $1.11, eventually hitting a multi-week peak of roughly $1.65. However, the bulls couldn't hold that level for long. The price was quickly rejected and has since settled back into the $1.55 range. Despite this pullback, XRP's price is showing significant strength against its competitors. Its market cap is holding steady above $90 billion, keeping it firmly in the race for the fourth-largest cryptocurrency by market value, just ahead of BNB (BNB-USD).

XRP ETFs Have Still Hit the $1.2 Billion Milestone

Despite the slow week, the bigger picture for XRP ETFs remains positive. Total net inflows have stabilized above $1.20 billion since the first products launched. Bitwise's XRP fund (XRP) and Franklin Templeton's XRPZ (XRPZ) follow closely behind Canary, with $360 million and $328 million in assets, respectively. While the green streak that lasted for the first month has finally broken, many analysts believe this is simply a period of consolidation as the market waits for a new catalyst to drive the next round of institutional adoption.

Investors can compare the XRP ETFs mentioned on the TipRanks ETF Comparison Tool. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Gas Station Operator EG Group Motors Towards $9B U.S. IPO
data.description:
Gas station operator EG Group is set for a $9 billion IPO in the U.S. by the summer.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Aiming to Raise $1B

According to a report in the Financial Times, the company founded by brothers Mohsin and Zuber Issa and backed by TDR Capital will look to raise about $1 billion when it lists in the first half of 2026. That will value the group at around $9 billion.

It is understood that banks including Bank of America (BAC) and Goldman Sachs (GS) are advising on the IPO. The listing is expected to take place under the Cumberland Farms name – the US forecourt retail operator EG owns in the U.S.

The gas forecourts giant founded in Blackburn, England, in 2001 offers Grocery & Merchandise, Foodservice, and Fuel services.

It has sites in 8 countries, including the U.S., and revenues of just over $24 billion. According to its website, it serves 2.5 million customers every day, and sells 180 million cups of coffee every year.

In total, EG Group employs about 33,000 people and operates roughly 4,300 sites. In recent years, EG has substantially reduced its borrowings by selling assets, including offloading the majority of its U.K. sites to Asda. The group has also sold its operations in Italy, and is close to doing so in Australia.

Healthy IPO Action

EG Group is roughly 50% owned by TDR Capital, the London-based private equity firm, which also owns a controlling stake in U.K. supermarket Asda. The brothers own 25% of the company each.

The U.S. is its biggest single market, and as such its global headquarters are moving to Charlotte in North Carolina. Its chief executive, Russell Colaco, is also based in the U.S.

It marks a healthy start to 2026 for U.S. IPO announcements as pent-up demand following the U.S. government shutdown last year bursts onto the market. Bitcoin miner BitGo (BTGO) and construction group EquipmentShare (EQPT) have already gone public. Other big names which might list this year include Anthropic and OpenAI.

However, the decision last week by brokerage Clear Street to postpone its IPO citing market conditions could cause others to think twice. Analysts said the Clear Street decision was down to a slump in crypto trading and the recent AI-driven selloff in financial and software stocks.

What Other IPOs are in the Pipeline?

data.author: David Craik

data.title: Apple Stock Forecast — Should You Catch this Falling Knife?
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) shares have dropped nearly 8% over the past five days after the company delayed its highly anticipated Siri AI upgrades. Investors are wary of repeated Siri setbacks and Apple's late AI entry.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A Bloomberg report noted that Apple's Siri update has hit testing snags, potentially delaying several features. Apple originally targeted iOS 26.4 for the March release but now plans phased rollouts across multiple updates. Some are expected with iOS 26.5 in May or even with iOS 27 in September.

Here's What Analysts Say

Evercore ISI analyst Amit Daryanani maintained his Buy rating and $330 price target (29% upside potential) despite news of the delay. The five-star analyst noted that testing issues have forced Siri to stick with its existing ChatGPT integration rather than Apple's new tech. He expects gradual feature rollouts starting mid-year.

iOS 26.5 will introduce a new web search tool and custom image generation. Additionally, iOS 27 brings a major AI overhaul, possibly integrating Google's (GOOGL) Gemini models. Daryanani also highlighted that Apple's Q1FY26 results beat expectations on sales and earnings, boosted by strong Services growth and low memory chip costs.

For the March quarter, Apple had guided for 13%-16% revenue growth, above prior estimates, backed by solid iPhone supply and continued Services momentum. The company is focusing on data privacy and smooth integration for its upcoming AI features.

Similarly, Robert W. Baird analyst William Power also reiterated his Buy rating and $300 price target, suggesting 17.3% upside potential. His price target suggests a 35x 2026 EPS forecast, sitting at the high end of Apple's post-COVID range (25-35x). AAPL stock valuation is also above peers in tech/consumer staples, but is justified by solid execution, rising Services revenue, ecosystem strength, and lower capital needs.

Meanwhile, Power sees risks such as component pricing and supply chain issues, regulatory and geopolitical pressures, competition from Google, Samsung (SSNLF), Microsoft (MSFT) as well as macroeconomic headwinds, changing consumer tastes, and high valuation.

Is AAPL a Good Stock to Buy?

Despite Siri delays causing an 8% five-day AAPL drop, analysts like Daryanani and Power maintain Buy ratings, viewing it as a calculated risk for long-term investors given strong Services growth and phased AI rollouts.

On TipRanks, Apple stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 17 Buys, nine Holds, and one Sell rating. The average Apple price target of $307.55 implies 20.2% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, AAPL shares have gained 4.6%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Analysts See Further Upside in Vertiv Stock (VRT) after Stellar Earnings
data.description:
While software stocks like Salesforce (CRM), ServiceNow (NOW), and Microsoft (MSFT) are being battered amid concerns about potential artificial intelligence (AI)-led disruption, shares of Vertiv Holdings (VRT) have rallied about 45% year to date, driven by robust demand for data center infrastructure. The provider of power and cooling solutions delivered impressive fourth-quarter results last week, with organic orders growing 252% year over year. Given a strong demand backdrop, analysts remain bullish on the AI infrastructure stock and see further upside potential.  

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Analysts Raise Price Targets for Vertiv Stock

In reaction to the impressive Q4 print, TD Cowen analyst Michael Elias raised the price target for Vertiv stock to $269 from $211 and reiterated a Buy rating. The 4-star analyst noted that Vertiv reported record Q4 2025 implied order volume of $8.4 billion, up from $3.7 billion in Q3 2025 and $2.3 billion in Q4 2024. Noting solid data center-led demand in the U.S., Elias continues to see upside for Vertiv through 2026, with his firm's checks indicating continued "positive price-cost," enhanced margins, and solid near-term order volumes, provided the company continues to enhance its capacity.

Elias views VRT as his "Best Idea" for 2026 based on the robust demand backdrop. The analyst highlighted that despite the large orders in Q4 2025, management expects continued growth, driven mainly by the demand in the Americas.

Likewise, Goldman Sachs analyst Mark Delaney raised the price target for Vertiv stock to $277 from $204 and reiterated a Buy rating following the impressive Q4 results. The 5-star analyst noted that Vertiv continues to be well-positioned for long-term growth tied to data center infrastructure, as reflected in solid Q4 orders.

Also, Morgan Stanley analyst Christopher Snyder boosted his price target to $285 from $200 and reaffirmed a Buy rating. The 4-star analyst is confident about the "durability" of Vertiv's revenue growth, innovation, and "inflecting" service opportunity.

Is VRT a Good Stock to Buy?

Overall, Wall Street is highly bullish on Vertiv Holdings stock, with a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 14 Buys and one Hold recommendation. The average VRT stock price target of $275 indicates 17.3% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Chevron Moves into Greek Waters with New Gas Exploration Agreements
data.description:
Chevron (CVX) is expanding its energy footprint in the eastern Mediterranean after a consortium led by the U.S. oil major signed exclusive agreements to explore for natural gas off southern Greece. The deal expands Greece's offshore exploration area and is the second recent agreement with a U.S. energy company, reflecting Europe's efforts to move away from Russian supplies and increase U.S. energy involvement.

data.title: Options Volatility and Implied Earnings Moves This Week, February 16 – February 19, 2026
data.description:
This week, several major companies will be reporting earnings. Among them are Walmart (WMT), Energy Transfer (ET), Palo Alto Networks (PANW), Occidental Petroleum (OXY), CoreWeave (CRWV).

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ahead of this week's major earnings announcements, TipRanks has calculated the expected moves of these companies' stocks. We use the at-the-money straddle of the options whose post-earnings expiration dates are closest to the earnings announcement date.

Note that options volatility tends to be high in the days leading up to an earnings announcement, due to uncertainty around the earnings. Investors don't know whether the earnings will be positive or negative, so the price of options (aka the option premium) rises.

Furthermore, after the earnings announcement, because the uncertainty has been resolved, the price of options (the option premium) decreases substantially. This is known as the volatility crush.
